lessons.title: Phone calls & messages
lessons.description: Learn to make phone calls and leave messages politely.

target_phrases: Could you please call me back?

tasks.question: If you want someone to call you again, you ask them to:
Can you ___ me back later?
You missed a call from your boss. What do you text?
options: Sorry I missed your call. I will call you back soon.

content.example_response: Hi, this is Priya. I am calling about the meeting. Please call me back when you are free.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Hello, is Sonal there?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: She is not available right now. Can I take a message?

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Yes, please ask her to call me back.

checklist: I can ask someone to call me back.
I can leave a voicemail.
I know phone call vocabulary.

lessons.title: Shopping clothes & returns
lessons.description: Learn to talk about sizes, fits, and how to return or exchange items.

example_sentences: Do you have this in a bigger size?
Can I exchange this for a different color?

target_phrases: I want to return this shirt.

tasks.question: If you want to give a shirt back and get your money, you ask for a:
Do you have this in a smaller ___?

pairs.right: Do you have a smaller size?

tasks.question: Staff says: 'Do you have the receipt?' You reply:

content.example_response: This shirt is too small. Do you have a medium size? I'd like to exchange it.

tasks.prompt: Roleplay: exchange for a different color

turns.speaker: Customer
turns.text: I'd like to exchange this dress.

turns.speaker: Staff
turns.text: Is there something wrong with it?

turns.speaker: Customer
turns.text: No, but I want it in blue, not red.

comprehension_questions.question: Why does the customer want to exchange the dress?
comprehension_questions.expected_answer: Because they want a different color.

checklist: I can ask for a different size or color.
I can return or exchange an item.
I can answer questions about receipts.

lessons.title: Eating out complaints & dietary needs
lessons.description: Learn to state dietary needs and make polite complaints at a restaurant.

example_sentences: Can I have this without onions?

target_phrases: Excuse me, my soup is cold.
Sorry, but this is not what I ordered.
Could you please heat this up?

tasks.question: You ordered chicken, but the waiter brought you fish. The problem is:
I am ___ to dairy.

tasks.answer: Excuse me this soup is cold

pairs.right: I will heat it up for you.

tasks.question: Staff asks: 'Is there anything you can’t eat?' You reply:

content.example_response: Excuse me. I ordered the pasta, but this is a salad. Could you please check my order?

turns.speaker: Customer
turns.text: I'd like the curry, but I'm allergic to peanuts.

turns.speaker: Staff
turns.text: I can ask the chef to make it without peanuts.

turns.speaker: Customer
turns.text: Thank you, that would be great.

comprehension_questions.question: What is the customer allergic to?

checklist: I can state my food allergies.
I can ask for food modifications.
I can politely complain about food.

lessons.title: Health & lifestyle
lessons.description: Talk about healthy habits and give basic advice for illnesses.

example_sentences: I exercise three times a week.

tasks.question: If you do something zero times, you ___ do it.
She ___ yoga every morning.
Your friend says: 'I feel stressed from work.' You say:

content.example_response: I usually wake up early and go for a walk. I eat healthy food and sleep for eight hours.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: I have a bad headache today.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Oh no. You should drink water and rest.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Thanks, I will do that.

checklist: I can talk about my healthy habits.
I can describe simple symptoms.
I can give advice using 'should'.

lessons.title: Directions
lessons.description: Ask for and give step-by-step directions using imperatives and common location/movement prepositions.

content.grammar_focus: Imperatives (Turn left / Go straight / Take the second right)
content.context: You are helping a friend in Chennai to find a pharmacy.
Finding places near a road and a park.

content.grammar_focus: How far / How long to get there
example_sentences: How far is the bus stop?
How long does it take to walk there?
content.context: Your friend asks how long the walk is.

tasks.prompt: Repeat: Turn left / Go straight / Take the second right
You want someone to turn. Choose the best instruction.
tasks.question: Choose: ___ at the corner.

tasks.prompt: Which option matches 'Walk past the bank'?
tasks.question: The word 'past' means…

tasks.prompt: How do you ask about distance?
tasks.question: Choose the correct question:

tasks.prompt: Pick the best instruction for reaching a place.
tasks.question: You want to say: 'Go straight for two minutes.' Choose the best option.
Go ___ the park.
Walk ___ the bank and turn right.

tasks.prompt: Reorder the words to make a correct instruction.
Reorder the words to make a correct instruction.
Match the preposition to the meaning in your directions.
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

dialogue.setting: Chennai, near a bus stop and a small park

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Hi Arjun! Excuse me, where is the pharmacy near here?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Sure. Go straight for two minutes.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Okay. After that?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Take the second right, and walk past the bank.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Past the bank... got it. Is it close?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: It’s about ten minutes on foot.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Do I go through the park?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Yes, go through the park, then go across the street.

turns.speaker: Priya
turns.text: Great! Thank you so much.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: No problem. You’ll see the pharmacy on the left.

comprehension_questions.question: Where does Priya want to go?
How long does Arjun say it takes to walk there?
What does Arjun say Priya should do after walking past the bank?
comprehension_questions.expected_answer: Go through the park and go across the street.

tasks.wrong_sentence: Go through the street and across the park.
tasks.correct_sentence: Go across the street and through the park.

tasks.prompt: Real-world choice: Your friend is lost. What should you say first?
tasks.question: You should say:
options: Turn left at the corner. (start directions)

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Give directions clearly using imperatives. Prompt: Say directions from a bus stop to a nearby temple. Example_response included. Key phrases included. Speak for 60-75 seconds.

content.example_response: Hi! To go to the temple, go straight from the bus stop. After two minutes, take the second right. Then walk past the bakery and go through the small lane. After that, cross the street and you will see the temple on the left. It is about ten or twelve minutes.

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Ask for confirmation. Prompt: Your friend follows your directions but is not sure. Ask two follow-up questions using 'How far'/'How long' and one yes/no question.

content.example_response: Hi! Are you near the park now? How long does it take from here? How far is the temple from your side? Tell me if you can see the signboard.

tasks.question: Complete the directions: Go ___ for two minutes. Then ___ the second right. Walk ___ the bank. Finally, go ___ the park and ___ the street.

checklist: I can use imperatives: Turn / Go / Take.
I can use movement prepositions: along / past / through / across.
I can ask: How far / How long?

lessons.title: Jobs & the workplace
lessons.description: Talk about job descriptions and what people are doing now at work using present continuous for current projects.

content.context: Talking about where people work in Mumbai.

tasks.prompt: Learn present continuous for current projects

content.grammar_focus: Present continuous (I’m currently working on… / She is preparing…)
example_sentences: I’m currently working on a new report.
She is preparing lunch for customers.
He is fixing a computer today.
content.context: Describing what someone is doing this week at work.

example_sentences: I talk to customers in the store.

target_phrases: I’m currently working on a new report.
He is fixing a computer today.

tasks.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'I work in a store.'
tasks.question: Which is correct?

tasks.prompt: Choose the correct present continuous form.
tasks.question: She ___ currently preparing orders.
A doctor works in a…

tasks.prompt: Pick the best question to ask about someone’s job.
tasks.question: You want to ask: where do you work?
I’m currently ___ on a new plan.
Meera ___ in a pharmacy.

tasks.prompt: Reorder the words to form a correct sentence.
tasks.answer: I’m currently working on a report

tasks.prompt: Reorder the words to form a correct sentence.
tasks.answer: Ravi is fixing the computer today

tasks.prompt: Match job roles to workplace actions.
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

dialogue.setting: Bangalore, inside an IT office and a meeting room

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Hi Kavya! What do you do here?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: Hi Arjun. I’m a software tester.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Nice! Are you busy today?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: Yes. I’m currently working on a new test case.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Great. Who helps you?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: My colleague Ravi. He is checking the bugs right now.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: What about your team?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: Our team works together. We discuss problems in the meeting room.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: So you are preparing reports too?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: Yes, I’m writing a summary for the manager.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Cool! Thanks for explaining.

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.text: No problem! See you later.

comprehension_questions.question: What is Kavya doing today?
comprehension_questions.expected_answer: She is currently working on a new test case and writing a summary.

comprehension_questions.question: Who is helping Kavya right now?

tasks.wrong_sentence: Ravi are fixing the computer today.
tasks.correct_sentence: Ravi is fixing the computer today.

tasks.prompt: Real-world choice: You want to describe what your coworker is doing now.
tasks.question: Choose the best sentence:
options: She is currently preparing customer orders.
She currently prepares customer orders every day.
She is prepare customer orders now.

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Describe your job today and what you are working on right now. Prompt: 'In an Indian context, tell about your workplace role.' Speak for 60-75 seconds.

content.example_response: Hi! I work in an office near my home. I’m currently working on a simple project for my manager. Today I’m checking files and writing notes for my team. My colleagues help me when I have questions. We work together and we share updates in the meeting room. This week I’m focusing on finishing the report, and then we will review it.

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Ask and answer about work. Prompt: Create a short mini-dialogue. Speak for 60 seconds.

content.example_response: A: Where do you work? B: I work in a pharmacy. A: What do you do there? B: I help customers and I check medicines. A: Are you busy now? B: Yes, I’m currently preparing orders. A: Who helps you? B: My colleague helps me and the team works together.

tasks.question: Complete: I ___ in a store. Today I’m ___ orders for customers. My ___ is Ravi, and he is ___ bugs right now. Our ___ works together in the team room.

checklist: I can talk about jobs with simple present (I work / She works).
I can talk about current work with present continuous (I’m working / He is fixing).
I know key workplace words like colleague, team, customer, office.

lessons.title: Weather & seasons (extended)
lessons.description: Extend weather language with present continuous for activities, plus will/going to for weather predictions and plans.

content.grammar_focus: Present continuous review (It’s raining. I’m staying home.)

example_sentences: It will rain in the afternoon.
The weather will be cold at night.
content.context: Talking about tomorrow’s weather in Chennai.

tasks.prompt: Learn going to for weather plans

content.grammar_focus: Going to + base verb (Plans based on weather)
example_sentences: I’m going to carry an umbrella.
We’re going to stay inside if it rains.
She’s going to wear a jacket.
content.context: Preparing for weather changes in Bangalore.

tasks.prompt: Repeat: It will... / I’m going to...

target_phrases: It will rain in the afternoon.
I’m going to carry an umbrella.

tasks.question: Tomorrow, it’s cloudy now. You want to say: It ___ sunny tomorrow.

tasks.prompt: Choose the correct present continuous sentence.
tasks.question: Right now, it’s raining. Choose:

tasks.prompt: Choose a going-to plan for weather.
tasks.question: If it is cold, you want to say: I’m going to ___ a jacket.

tasks.prompt: Choose the best sentence: prediction vs plan
tasks.question: It looks very dark. The rain is possible soon. Choose:
options: It’s going to rain soon? (ungrammatical here)
It is going to rain now.

tasks.question: It ___ be hot this weekend.
I’m going to ___ an umbrella.

tasks.prompt: Reorder to make a correct sentence (prediction).
Reorder to make a correct sentence (plan).
tasks.answer: She’s going to wear a jacket

tasks.prompt: Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

dialogue.setting: Chennai, outside a local shop after work

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: Arjun, why are you holding an umbrella?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Because the weather is changing. It’s cloudy now.

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: So, will it rain today?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Yes, I think it will rain this evening.

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: What are you going to do?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: I’m going to stay near home after work.

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: We have plans to meet at 7.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: If it rains, we’re going to meet in a café.

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: That’s smart. Are you cold?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Not really, but I’m wearing a light jacket.

turns.speaker: Meera
turns.text: Okay. Let’s message each other at 6:30.

comprehension_questions.question: Why is Arjun holding an umbrella?
comprehension_questions.expected_answer: Because the weather is changing and it may rain.

comprehension_questions.question: What does Arjun think will happen this evening?
Where are they going to meet if it rains?

tasks.wrong_sentence: It will rain now, so I’m staying inside.
tasks.correct_sentence: It is raining now, so I’m staying inside.

tasks.prompt: Real-world choice: You see dark clouds. What should you say?
tasks.question: Choose the best option:
options: It is going to rain soon (as a general fact).

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Describe today’s weather and your plan for tomorrow. Include: one present continuous sentence, one 'will' prediction, one 'going to' plan. Speak 60-75 seconds.

content.example_response: Today it’s raining a little, and I’m staying at home in the evening. Tomorrow it will be sunny, so I’m going to go for a walk in the morning. I’m also going to wear comfortable clothes. If the weather changes again, I will stay inside for some time. I think the weekend will be nice for the park.

tasks.prompt: Instruction: Make a short weather conversation. Prompt: Ask a friend about what they will do if it rains. Speak 60 seconds.

content.example_response: Meera: It’s cloudy. Will it rain? Arjun: Yes, I think it will rain soon. Meera: What will you do? Arjun: I’m going to carry an umbrella and I’m going to meet you at a café if it rains. Meera: Okay, let’s message at 6:30.

tasks.question: It’s ___ now. I’m ___ home. Tomorrow, it will be sunny. I’m going to ___ a walk in the evening, but if it ___, I’m going to stay inside.

checklist: I can use present continuous for weather right now.
I can predict: It will…
I can plan: I’m going to… (based on weather).

lessons.title: Unit 6 Final Review + A1 Exit Test
lessons.description: Mixed review of Unit 6 skills: phone/messages, shopping & returns, restaurant complaints & dietary needs, health & lifestyle, directions, jobs, and weather. Includes a final speaking test.

content.grammar_focus: Polite request forms (Can you… / Would you…?)
example_sentences: Can you send the address, please?
Would you like to confirm the time?

tasks.prompt: Quick review: Present continuous (now at work / weather)

example_sentences: I’m currently working on a report.

target_phrases: I’m currently working on a report.

tasks.prompt: Shopping/returns: Which is best for an exchange request?
tasks.question: I want to exchange this shirt because it doesn’t fit. I need…

tasks.prompt: Restaurant dietary needs: Pick the correct sentence.
tasks.question: Choose the correct way to say a restriction.

tasks.prompt: Health & lifestyle: Choose the best advice phrase.
tasks.question: If someone is tired, you say:
options: You should speak to the weather.

tasks.question: Choose the prediction: Tomorrow it ___ windy.
Walk ___ the bank and turn right.
Ravi is currently ___ a new schedule.

tasks.answer: Could you please send me the address?

tasks.prompt: Match situations to the best response style.

pairs.right: Use I’m allergic to… / I can’t eat…

tasks.prompt: Final mixed dialogue (Unit 6). Read and answer.

dialogue.setting: Mumbai, outside a metro station and nearby café

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: Hi Arjun! I have a message problem. Can you help me?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Sure. What happened?

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: My phone is not working. Could you call me back when you can?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Okay. Also, you look hungry. Are you going to the café?

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: Yes, but I’m allergic to peanuts. I can’t eat any peanuts.

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: No problem. What about the route? The café is near the station.

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: How do I get there from here?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: Go straight for two minutes. Take the second right, and walk past the pharmacy.

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: It’s raining now. Will it get worse?

turns.speaker: Arjun
turns.text: I think it will stop soon. I’m going to wait here, and then we can go together.

turns.speaker: Sonal
turns.text: Great! Let’s meet inside after 10 minutes.

comprehension_questions.question: What does Sonal ask Arjun to do?
What does Arjun say about the weather?
comprehension_questions.expected_answer: He thinks it will stop soon.

tasks.wrong_sentence: I’m currently work on a new report.
tasks.correct_sentence: I’m currently working on a new report.

tasks.prompt: Exit test: You want an exchange at a store. Choose best first line.
tasks.question: What should you say?
options: Hello, I want to make an exchange. It doesn’t fit.
Hello, turn left at the receipt.

tasks.prompt: A1 Final Speaking Test (Part 1): Directions + confirmation. Instruction: Speak for 60-80 seconds. Include: 1 request for directions, 2 steps using imperatives, and 1 confirmation question.

content.example_response: Excuse me, can you help me? I want to find a pharmacy near here. Go straight for two minutes. Then take the second right and walk past the bank. After that, go through the park. How far is it from here? Is it on the left?

tasks.prompt: A1 Final Speaking Test (Part 2): Workplace + weather + dietary needs (choose one each if needed). Speak for 60-80 seconds.

content.example_response: Hi! I work at a small office. Today I’m currently working on a report for my manager. Right now it’s raining outside, so I’m staying inside. Also, I have a diet need: I’m allergic to peanuts, so I can’t eat food with peanuts. If we go out later, we will choose a place with safe food.

tasks.question: Complete: Could you ___ me back? I’m allergic ___ peanuts. Go straight ___ two minutes. It ___ rain tomorrow. I’m currently working ___ a new test case.

checklist: I can handle basic phone/message requests.
I can shop and ask for size/returns changes.
I can express dietary needs and polite restaurant issues.
I can give and ask for directions.
I can talk about my job and what I’m doing now.
I can talk about weather with predictions and plans.
